content.claim: Teacher shortages are greatest in rural areas, with proportion of unqualified teachers in disadvantaged schools 2-3 times higher than in non-disadvantaged schools, and nationally half of all teachers are 50 or older while only 7.2% are under 30 in 2024/2025
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Staffing quality data establish that 'access' to a village school does not guarantee equal teaching quality
Age and qualification distributions are national and do not isolate rural-specific teacher quality effects
Causal relationship between teacher composition and student outcomes not directly measured
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
